meta_title: For Freelancers: debugging with AI to Solve stuck with basic apps (Intermediate Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to debugging with AI and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Intermediate-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: Debugging with AI: The Freelancer's Guide to Complex Apps
content.seo_description: Break through development plateaus. Learn how to leverage Antigravity and Claude Code to debug complex SaaS applications and scale your freelance output.
content.answer_block: Stop manually tracing logs. By integrating Claude Code for context-aware analysis and Antigravity for automated dependency mapping, you transform debugging from a bottleneck into a competitive advantage. This systematic approach allows you to identify root causes in minutes, not hours, ensuring your freelance projects ship on time and bug-free.
content.intro:
You know the feeling: you’re three weeks into a client build, and a phantom state-sync issue is turning your high-velocity sprint into a slow-motion crawl. The codebase, once a source of pride, now feels like a labyrinth of technical debt. You aren't just stuck; you're losing billable hours to the 'debugging abyss.'

But the era of manual console-logging is over. By weaponizing AI-native development tools, you can offload the cognitive load of complex system architecture. We aren't just fixing bugs; we are engineering a resilient, self-documenting workflow that separates the hobbyists from the high-earning freelance architects.

steps.title: Establish Contextual Baselines
steps.description: Before debugging, you must map the current state. Use AI to index your codebase so the model understands the architectural intent versus the actual runtime behavior.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze the current project structure and identify potential bottlenecks in the state management layer. Create a dependency graph of the affected module.
steps.claude_code_command: claude add . --ignore node_modules && claude ask 'Explain the data flow in the user-auth module.'

steps.title: Automated Error Surface Mapping
steps.description: Feed your error logs into the AI to pinpoint the exact intersection of failing logic and data input.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Compare these error logs against the existing API schema and identify the mismatch causing the 500 error.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'grep -r "error" ./logs' --reasoning 'Identify the stack trace root cause.'

steps.title: Simulate Edge-Case Scenarios
steps.description: Don't just fix the bug; build a test harness. Generate unit tests that explicitly target the failure point to prevent regression.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Jest test suite that mocks the failing API response and asserts the correct handler behavior.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write 'Create a unit test for the auth service to handle null token responses.'

steps.title: Refactor for Defensive Programming
steps.description: Apply AI-driven refactoring to wrap the fragile code in robust error handling blocks.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor this function to implement a circuit breaker pattern and add exhaustive logging for production troubleshooting.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit src/services/auth.ts --instructions 'Implement circuit breaker pattern and add error logging.'

steps.title: Final Architectural Verification
steps.description: Perform a final scan to ensure the fix hasn't introduced side effects in interconnected services.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Audit the recent changes against the entire codebase for breaking changes in shared interfaces.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Review the recent changes for potential regression risks in the database schema.'

pro_tips: Always use 'context-only' mode in Claude Code for large repos to prevent token overflow.
Treat your Antigravity dependency maps as living documentation for your clients.
Never accept the first AI-generated fix; always ask it to 'provide three alternative implementation strategies'.
Use CLI aliases for your most common debugging prompts to save 15+ minutes per session.
Force the AI to explain the 'why' behind the bug to build your own intuition for future builds.
resources: Effective Prompt Engineering for Code: https://anthropic.com/news/claude-code
content.next_steps: Integrate these CLI tools into your CI/CD pipeline to automate the first pass of debugging before you even open your editor.
content.conversion_block: Stop fighting your code and start mastering your architecture. Elevate your freelance business with elite debugging workflows. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
